t_ch: 你瞧，它又来了。我都懒得去拦…咦，这次还不一样？
t_en: Look, it's coming again. I don't even bother stopping it anymore... Huh? Something's different this time.

t_ch: 失恋的大地兽再次从你们面前呼啸而过，只不过现在多了好几个跟在后面的人。
t_en: The heartbroken dromas charges past once more, but now it's followed by several people trailing behind.

t_ch: 列队！齐步跑！紧跟殿下的步伐！
t_en: Line up! March in step! Keep pace with His Highness!

t_ch: 机会啊！好不容易找到和它相关的人了，走，我们去把他们拦下来。
t_en: This is our chance! We finally found someone connected to it. Let's stop them.

t_ch: 砰——你再次感受到了天旋地转的滋味。但还未等你倒下，便被结实的胳膊扶住了。
t_en: Bang — You feel the world spin once again, but before you can fall, a strong arm catches you.

t_ch: 抱歉啊，我们在这里跑操，不小心撞倒了你。没什么大事吧？
t_en: Sorry about that. We were jogging here and accidentally knocked you over. Are you alright?

t_ch: 喂，对奥赫玛人这么友善干嘛？他们在排挤悬锋人的时候，可没这么好的语气！
t_en: Hey, why are you being so kind to these Okhemans? When they were ostracizing the Kremnoans, they weren't using such nice words!

t_ch: 但确实是我们有错在先，若非是科可波阁下奔跑的速度过快，这位公民也不会受伤。
t_en: True, we were at fault first. If not for Kokopo's incredible speed, this citizen wouldn't have been injured.

t_ch: 悬锋人不会停下。击碎挡在前方的所有东西，是流淌在我们血液里的本能！
t_en: Kremnoans don't stop for anyone. Breaking through everything in our path — that's what flows through our veins!

t_ch: 好吧，你想你已经明白这群人的身份了。
t_en: Alright, you think you've figured out who these people are.

t_ch: 你知道的，万敌是我兄弟。
t_en: You know, Mydei is a brother to me.

t_ch: 万敌刚走就闹事是吧？！
t_en: So Mydei leaves, and now you're stirring up trouble!?

t_ch: 咦？！啊…原来你是殿下的朋友，那就好说话了。对不起，是我们的错。
t_en: Huh!? Oh... so you're a friend of His Highness? That's different. Sorry, this was our mistake.

t_ch: 那又如何？殿下是交代过我们要和奥赫玛人友善相处，可这难道意味着我们要步步退让，苟且偷生，丢掉悬锋人的尊严和荣耀吗？！
t_en: And what of it? His Highness instructed us to treat the Okhemans amicably, but does that mean we must retreat with every step, live dishonorably, and abandon the dignity and glory of the Kremnoans!?

t_ch: 先就事论事吧。殿下的朋友，自然也是我们的朋友。别失了礼数，让异乡客人以为我们是只懂得作战的莽夫。
t_en: Let's focus on the matter at hand. A friend of His Highness is naturally our friend as well. Don't lose your manners, or the foreign guest might think we're nothing but brutes who only know how to fight.

t_ch: 所以…呃，我插句话，你们就是这头大地兽的主人？
t_en: So... uh, let me interrupt. Are you the owners of this dromas?

t_ch: 不是。科可波阁下的主人从来只有一位，那便是万敌殿下。
t_en: No. Kokopo belongs to only one master, and that is His Highness, Mydei.

t_ch: 详细地说，科可波阁下的全名为「悬锋猛兽•科可波三世」，乃是陪伴过万敌殿下四处征战的坐骑，骁勇善战，勇冠三军，锐不可当。
t_en: To be precise, Kokopo's full name is "The Fierce Beast of Kremnos: Kokopo III." It was the loyal steed of Lord Mydei, accompanying him through countless battles — valiant, peerless in combat, and utterly unstoppable.

t_ch: 只是，我们的王离开后，科可波阁下的性情，也因深重思念而变得反常……
t_en: But ever since our king left, Kokopo's temperament has grown erratic, burdened by profound longing...

t_ch: 思念是一种病啊！
t_en: Longing is a sickness!

t_ch: 确实。古语说道，孤独是足以致死的疾病，想必科可波阁下也是念主心切，外加不适应城内的安逸环境，才会做出这等狂放的行径吧。
t_en: Indeed. The old saying goes, "Loneliness is a fatal ailment." It seems Kokopo, too, longs deeply for its master. Struggling to adapt to the tranquil life within the city walls has only exacerbated its restlessness.

t_ch: 呵，我们…这些被留下来的悬锋人，也何尝不是如此呢？
t_en: Heh. And we, the Kremnoans left behind — aren't we the same?

t_ch: 那你们在干嘛？
t_en: So what are you doing now?

t_ch: 我们…哎，让你看笑话了。
t_en: We... made a fool of ourselves in front of you.

t_ch: 古语说，孤独是足以致死的疾病，想必科可波阁下也是念主心切，外加不适应城内的安逸环境，才会做出这等狂放的行径。
t_en: The old saying goes, "Loneliness is a fatal ailment." It seems Kokopo, too, longs deeply for its master. Struggling to adapt to the tranquil life within the city walls has only exacerbated its restlessness.

t_ch: 呵，而我们…这些被留下来的悬锋人，也何尝不是如此呢？
t_en: Heh. And we, the Kremnoans left behind — aren't we the same?

t_ch: 悬锋人生来便与征战相伴，兵器是我们的手足，盔甲是我们的华服。可现在，让我们放下所有荣耀，拿起锄头与算盘…我没法轻易做到。
t_en: Born into a life of war, weapons are like our limbs, and armor is our finest attire. But now, we Kremnoans are told to put down our glory, pick up hoes and abacuses... It's not something I can easily accept.

t_ch: 对故乡的眷念，对价值的渴求，对王的依赖，还有被迫融入死敌城邦的隔阂…我们只有继续跟在科可波阁下的身后奔跑，才能稍微化解心头的苦闷。
t_en: Yearning for our homeland, for meaning, for our king... Being forced to integrate into the city of our enemies — there's a gap too wide to bridge. Only by continuing to run after Kokopo can we soothe the turmoil in our hearts.

t_ch: 就好像，大家仍在随殿下作战那般……
t_en: It's as if... we're still following His Highness into battle...

t_ch: 嗡嗡嗡……
t_en: Grumphhh...

t_ch: 哎，身处和平环境的人们，又如何能明白这样的心境呢？他们只会觉得悬锋人好斗、扰民、性情暴躁，动不动就将「战死」挂在嘴边，却不知道……
t_en: Also, how could those who've always lived in peace understand this? To them, Kremnoans are just belligerent troublemakers, prone to anger and always talking of "dying in battle." But they don't realize...

t_ch: 在这样的世界中，能平静活着便是种奢侈。而悬锋的王为他们扛起了重量。
t_en: In such a world, being able to live peacefully is a luxury. And the King of Kremnos bore that weight for them.

t_ch: 别太伤感了。
t_en: Don't get too sentimental.

t_ch: 是啊，这种「纷争」的后遗症，总需要时间去克服。只不过……
t_en: True. It's just the scars of Strife — something that takes time to heal. However...

t_ch: 懂了，你们是在找代餐。
t_en: Got it, you're just looking for a substitute.

t_ch: 不如说是「纷争」的后遗症吧，总需要时间去克服。只不过……
t_en: More like the scars of Strife... but it takes time to heal. But...

t_ch: 不用克服，悬锋人会找到自己的道路。走吧，科可波阁下，我们回家。
t_en: There's no need to heal. We Kremnoans will find our path. Come on, Kokopo, let's go home.

t_ch: 去找我们的王，去需要我们的地方。这里不欢迎，我们离开便是。
t_en: Let's find our king, and go to where we're needed. If we're unwelcome here, then we'll leave.

t_ch: ——奥赫玛的城门口，我会带着科可波阁下在那等你们，想随我返乡的，就自己来吧。悬锋城不会拒绝勇士，但也不欢迎懦弱之徒。
t_en: At the gates of Okhema, I'll wait with Kokopo. Come to join me if you want to return. Castrum Kremnos welcomes warriors but does not tolerate the cowards.

t_ch: 嗡嗡…嗡嗡嗡！
t_en: Grumphh... Grumphhh!

t_ch: 啧，悬锋族人的命运确实可悲可叹。但现在，我总算搞明白了一件事——
t_en: Tch. The fate of the Kremnoans is indeed tragic and lamentable. But now, you finally realize one thing —

t_ch: 你忘了找他们要钱？
t_en: You forgot to ask them for money?

t_ch: 我的赔偿金呢！
t_en: Where's my compensation?

t_ch: 甚至还被多撞了一次。
t_en: And I even got hit twice.

t_ch: 原来…<color=#dbc291ff>那头大地兽的恋爱对象，就是万敌啊！</color>
t_en: So... <color=#dbc291ff>that dromas' true love is none other than Mydei!</color>

t_ch: …就只有这些人？
t_en: ...Is that all?

t_ch: 要不…再想想？返乡的路途遥远，城外还有游荡的怪物，我担心你们的安全。
t_en: Maybe... think it over again? The journey home is long, and there are monsters outside the city. I'm worried about your safety.

t_ch: 别想了！我就算死在外面，也不愿在异邦的庇护下苟且偷生。悬锋的古训都被你们丢到脑后了吗，在那温热的浴池里泡得软烂不堪了吗？！
t_en: No more thinking! I'd rather die outside than survive under the protection of a foreign land. Have you forgotten the ancient creed of Kremnos? Have you soaked in those warm baths so long that you've gone soft!?

t_ch: 可这是王的意志！你要公然违背万敌殿下的命令吗？
t_en: But this is the will of our king! Are you going to openly defy Lord Mydei's orders?

t_ch: 殿下选择独自返乡，成为整个翁法罗斯的守护者，不就是希望我们能过上和平的生活，不必再与刀剑为伴吗！为什么非得要为那传统流血才行？
t_en: His Highness chose to return home alone and became the Guardian of Amphoreus so that we could live peacefully, free from the company of blades. Why must we bleed for tradition to prove ourselves?

t_ch: 那你倒是告诉我，该怎么在这活下去啊？！我只学过挥剑的本事，菜刀我使不来，更受不了向客人谄媚的生活。我是战士，不是个卖笑的！
t_en: Then tell me — how do we live here!? All I know is how to wield a sword. I can't handle a kitchen knife, let alone a life of flattering guests. I'm a warrior, not some grinning entertainer!

t_ch: 不管有多少人愿意跟上来，我已经决意要回家了。走，科可波阁下。
t_en: No matter how many choose to follow, I've made up my mind — I'm going home.

t_ch: 嗡嗡！
t_en: Grumph!

t_ch: 既然…我们都将科可波阁下视作殿下的化身，离开了大地兽的帮助，你也难以抵达悬锋城，不如…未来的路，就交由它来决定吧。
t_en: Since we all regard Kokopo as His Highness's embodiment, you won't reach Castrum Kremnos without its help. Perhaps... let it decide the path ahead.

t_ch: 异乡的勇士，万敌殿下的同伴，请你为悬锋族人见证此行的结局——
t_en: As a warrior from the outlands, a companion of Lord Mydei, please bear witness to our choice —

t_ch: 我只想拿到赔偿金。
t_en: I just want my compensation.

t_ch: 这是什么新型的占卜仪式吗？
t_en: Is this some new kind of divination ceremony?

t_ch: 科可波阁下，听我诉说——我知道悬锋族人在奥赫玛过得极为不易，也深知适应绝非一朝一夕便能完成，但这是我们必须走的路。
t_en: Kokopo, hear my plea — I know the Kremnoans struggle in Okhema, and adaptation isn't something achieved overnight. But this is the path we must walk.

t_ch: 悬锋的王在遥远处浴血奋战，我们理应支持他的决定，为他免除后顾之忧。
t_en: The King of Kremnos is far away, shedding blood for us. We should respect his will and ease his burdens.

t_ch: 现在返乡绝非勇猛之举，反而是懦弱的行径——那是从殿下委托的重任前、从新的战场中逃走，拒绝改变，贪图舒适，害怕无法掌控的未来。
t_en: Returning home now isn't an act of courage, it's one of cowardice — an escape from His Highness' entrusted mission, from this new battlefield. It's a refusal to change, a retreat into comfort, a fear of an uncontrollable future.

t_ch: 悬锋人的字典里，没有*恐惧*二字。以前没有，往后也不应该有。这才是我们的传统，我们能自称为「悬锋族人」的根基。
t_en: In the Kremnoan creed, there is no fear. There never was, and there never should be. That is our tradition, the foundation of our identity as Kremnoans.

t_ch: 科可波阁下，请做出你的判断吧——
t_en: Kokopo, make your choice —

t_ch: 嗡嗡嗡……
t_en: Grumphhh...

t_ch: 大地兽望向故乡的方向，发出低沉的悲鸣，可回应它的只有淡漠的云，以及空洞的风。
t_en: The dromas gazes towards the direction of its homeland, letting out a low, mournful cry. But the only response is indifferent clouds and hollow winds.

t_ch: 不知道在这里，它能否听到悬锋城内正响起的厮杀声？在深沉的睡眠中，它是否也曾梦到过与主人共同征战的时光？是否仍觉得心神激荡，想再回到那个时候？
t_en: Here, can it still hear the sounds of battle ringing out in Castrum Kremnos? In its deep sleep, has it dreamed of warring alongside its master? Does it still feel the stir of its soul, yearning to return to those days?

t_ch: 嗡嗡嗡……
t_en: Grumphhh...

t_ch: 大地兽望向身后的悬锋族人，那是曾随它的主人出生入死过的战士。可无论如何用荣誉和尊严做包装，他们都是活生生的、会受伤会流血的人们。
t_en: The dromas turns to look at the Kremnoans behind it — warriors who had fought alongside its master. No matter how they dressed their wounds in honor and pride, they are still people who bled and felt pain.

t_ch: 它和他们都还有很长的路要走，长到连{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}万径之门{RUBY_E#}都难以给出准确的衡量。因为生活绝非神的权能，而是人的意志。
t_en: They have a long road ahead of them — so long that not even the {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Gate of Infinity{RUBY_E#} could measure it precisely. Because life isn't divine providence — it's human resolve.

t_ch: 嗡嗡嗡……
t_en: Grumphhh...

t_ch: 加油！失恋的大地兽，要带着虔诚的心来认真做决定才行啊！
t_en: Come on, heartbroken dromas, you've got to make a serious decision with a sincere heart!

t_ch: 喂，别破坏气氛好吗？
t_en: Hey, read the room, will ya!

t_ch: 你怎么还在。
t_en: Why are you still here?

t_ch: 瞬间，清亮的啼鸣打破了现场的沉寂。
t_en: In that instant, a clear cry pierced the silence.

t_ch: 嗡嗡！呜嗡！呜呜嗡！呜嗡嗡！呜嗡嗡！呜——
t_en: Grumph! Grumph! Grumphhh! Grumphhh! Grumphhhh! Grumph —

t_ch: 大地兽做出了选择。它转身朝向奥赫玛的城门，头也不回地走去。
t_en: The dromas has made its choice. It turns toward Okhema's gates, walking away without looking back.

t_ch: 这样么。第二次命令，我也没有继续违抗的道理了。到此为止吧。
t_en: So that's it. The second command — I have no reason to disobey further. It ends here.

t_ch: 或许你可以去工坊找份新工作，陪科可波阁下散步的活可不是谁都能干的。
t_en: Perhaps you can find work in the workshop. Walking Kokopo isn't a job just anyone can do.

t_ch: 再见了，殿下，我们的王……
t_en: Farewell, Your Highness. Our king...

t_ch: 希望所有悬锋人，都能顺利找到自己的新生活。尽管很困难，但若是从最开始就想着逃走，逃回熟悉的地方，必然不会有改变。
t_en: May every Kremnoan find a new life. Though it's difficult, fleeing back to familiarity from the start ensures no change will ever come.

t_ch: 也感谢你的见证，愿{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}捍卫你。
t_en: And thank you for witnessing this. May the {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} watch over you.

t_ch: 呜呜…失恋的大地兽，你好温柔……
t_en: Oooh... heartbroken dromas, you're so gentle...

t_ch: 呜呜…太感动了，太好哭了，我终于明白了——
t_en: Oooh... I'm so moved, so tearful... I finally understand —

t_ch: 你变成大地兽了？
t_en: You've turned into a dromas?

t_ch: 不会还要讲大地兽的爱情吧？
t_en: Are we about to hear more about the dromas' love story?

t_ch: 原来…<color=#dbc291ff>那头大地兽的恋爱对象，是悬锋城啊！</color>
t_en: Turns out... <color=#dbc291ff>the dromas' true love is Castrum Kremnos!</color>

t_ch: 你是来探望科可波三世的吧？来得正好，看——那些悬锋人给它准备了什么！
t_en: You're here to check on Kokopo III, right? Perfect timing — look what the Kremnoans have prepared for him!

t_ch: 你惊奇地发现那头号称「全奥赫玛最美」的大地兽再次出现在了工坊里，而科可波三世则站在它身旁，用充满柔情蜜意的语气轻哼着。
t_en: You're surprised to find the dromas once hailed as the most beautiful in all Okhema back in the workshop, with Kokopo III standing beside her, humming in a tone filled with tenderness and affection.

t_ch: 嗡嗡…呜嗡嗡！
t_en: Grumph... Grumphhh!

t_ch: 嗡嗡~嗡嗡嗡~嗡嗡~
t_en: Grumph~ Grumphhh~ Grumph~

t_ch: 嗡嗡！呜嗡嗡嗡~
t_en: Grumph! Grumphhh~

t_ch: …好吧，是你最讨厌的，歪歪腻腻的幸福模样。
t_en: ...Well, it's that disgustingly sweet image of happiness you hate the most.

t_ch: 不管悬锋人的问题能否得到处理，但至少你解决了一头失恋大地兽的烦恼，不是吗？
t_en: No matter whether the Kremnoans' issues are resolved, at least you've helped this heartbroken dromas find some peace, haven't you?

t_ch: 科可波三世，你一定要幸福啊……
t_en: Kokopo III, you must be happy...

t_ch: 科可波阁下，在这过得还习惯吗，要不要再给你找点红土来？
t_en: Kokopo, is life here to your liking? Should I bring you more redsoil?

t_ch: 哼，还是悬锋城的土质尝起来更美味，对吧？
t_en: Hmph, but Kremnos' soil must taste better, right?

t_ch: （那群悬锋族人真打算返乡吗…去奥赫玛的城门口看看吧。）
t_en: (Are those Kremnoans really planning to go back? Maybe I should check the gates of Okhema.)

t_ch: …真要回去吗？
t_en: ...Are you really leaving?

t_ch: 必须听从王的命令。
t_en: We must obey the king's command.

t_ch: 那里才是我们的家！
t_en: That's where our home is!

t_ch: 我们不属于奥赫玛。
t_en: We don't belong to Okhema.

t_ch: 可我挺喜欢浴场的…
t_en: But I quite like the baths...

t_ch: …嗯？找我有事吗？
t_en: ...Oh? Looking for something?

t_ch: 我擅长替任何人代写情诗，有需求的话，随时来找我就好哦。
t_en: I specialize in ghostwriting love poems. If you need one, feel free to come and ask anytime.

t_ch: 蕾莉亚！我亲爱的，噢！你那美丽的双眸怎么不再闪动着明媚的光？
t_en: Lelia! My love! Oh, why have those beautiful eyes of yours lost their radiant glow?

t_ch: 蕾莉亚！快回应我！
t_en: Lelia! Say something, please!

t_ch: 亲爱的……
t_en: My love...

t_ch: 蕾莉亚！快振作起来！
t_en: Lelia! Stay with me!

t_ch: 埃德加…我…这一生过得很幸福。
t_en: Edgar... I... had a wonderful life.

t_ch: 蕾莉亚——！
t_en: LELIA—!

t_ch: 呜啊啊啊啊！
t_en: NOOOO!

t_ch: 发生什么事了？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 发生什么事了，是啊，我也曾想这样大声地问！
t_en: "What happened?" *bitter laugh* I asked myself the same question that day, screaming it until my voice gave out.

t_ch: 为什么你们只是看着？
t_en: Why are you just standing by and watching?

t_ch: 是啊，你们这些铁石心肠的看客！为什么只是看着！
t_en: Yeah, you heartless or something? Why are you just watching!?

t_ch: 我是不是该离开了…
t_en: Should I leave...?

t_ch: 不行！今天谁都不许离开！我要残酷的命运血债血偿！
t_en: No! No one is leaving today! I want the cruel fate to pay my debts with blood!

t_ch: 从出生起，我被丢弃在海岸礁石之上；自记事起，我便度过了痛苦的童年时光！
t_en: From birth, I was abandoned on a rocky shore. For as long as I can remember, my childhood is miserable!

t_ch: 后来我接受了悬锋城最严厉的选拔，却目睹与我交好的战士们都在战场上死去！
t_en: Later, I underwent the harshest trials in Castrum Kremnos, only to witness my comrades fall one by one on the battlefield!

t_ch: 在黑夜的纷乱中，我杀死了一位瘦削的老妇！
t_en: In the chaos of the night, I killed a frail old woman!

t_ch: 后来我才知道，那是我从未谋面的母亲！啊——！
t_en: Only later did I learn she was my estranged mother! Ahhh—!

t_ch: 而如今我又惨遭陷害，亲手杀害了心爱的妻子，啊！我的手上满是悔恨的鲜血！
t_en: And now, I've been entrapped, forced to murder my beloved wife! Ah! My hands are soaked in regretful blood!

t_ch: 陷害？你被谁陷害了？
t_en: Entrapped? Who set you up?

t_ch: 是神明！是那些高高在上的泰坦！
t_en: The gods! Those great Titans!

t_ch: 呜啊，好惨的一个人。
t_en: Ugh, such a tragic man.

t_ch: 是神明！害我众叛亲离，妻离子散……
t_en: The gods! They've left me isolated, betrayed, and bereft...

t_ch: （悲悯地沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent in sorrow)

t_ch: 你这丑恶的神明！我为你奉献了一切！你曾经多少次舐过我的祭品！
t_en: You despicable deity! I gave you everything! How many times have you enjoyed my offerings!

t_ch: 我曾在庄严的夜里，在烈火祭坛上献上食物，那是不能与别的泰坦共享的祭礼！
t_en: I once, in solemn nights, placed food upon the blazing altar, offerings not to be shared with other Titans!

t_ch: 哎，你这熟睡的神明，从不怜悯我的痛苦！
t_en: Yet you, slumbering deity, show no pity for my suffering!

t_ch: 为什么？！
t_en: Why!?

t_ch: 为什么神明要将这*不公平的命运*加诸于我的身上？
t_en: Why must the gods impose this unfair fate upon me?

t_ch: 我问你，你说为什么？
t_en: I ask you — why?

t_ch: 我不知道，我只是{RUBY_B#大地之泰坦}吉奥里亚{RUBY_E#}祝福的旅人。
t_en: I don't know... I'm just a traveler blessed by {RUBY_B#Earth Titan}Georios{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 你呢，你说为什么？
t_en: And you, what do you say?

t_ch: 我不知道，我只是{RUBY_B#浪漫之泰坦}墨涅塔{RUBY_E#}祝福的诗人。
t_en: I don't know... I'm merely a poet blessed by {RUBY_B#Romance Titan}Mnestia{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 那你呢！为什么？
t_en: And you! Why?

t_ch: 我是阿基维利祝福的无名客。
t_en: I am one of the Nameless blessed by Akivili.

t_ch: …咱们有这么个泰坦吗？
t_en: ...Do we even have a Titan called that?

t_ch: 因为神明很器重你，这是试炼。
t_en: Because the gods value you. This is a trial.

t_ch: 它凭什么给我试炼，我不要试炼！
t_en: What gave them the rights to make me go through trials? I don't want trials!

t_ch: 突然问我为什么，我不知道啊…
t_en: Why do you ask me so suddenly? I don't know!

t_ch: 没人知道！
t_en: No one knows!

t_ch: 没人能告诉我为什么！我好恨！我好恨啊！
t_en: No one can tell me why! I hate it! I hate it so much!

t_ch: 神明为什么要创造这样无情的世界，我恨创造出这样世界的神明……
t_en: Why did they create such a merciless world? I hate those who made this world...
I want to...

t_ch: 「我要…弑神！」
t_en: I want to slay the gods!

t_ch: …埃德加怎么跑了？剧本上没有写这段啊？他这人，戏好多……
t_en: ...Why did Edgar run off? This wasn't in the script. He's always being so dramatic...

t_ch: 他要干什么？我们还是赶紧追上去吧，那家伙好像还没从戏里出来！
t_en: What's he up to? We should catch up! He doesn't seem to have snapped out of the role!

t_ch: 什么情况，还排练不排练了？
t_en: What's going on? Are we still rehearsing?

t_ch: 伊俄那家伙大嚷着「我要弑神」就冲出去了…不会出事吧？
t_en: Eio stormed off yelling, "I want to slay the gods!"... This won't end well, right?

t_ch: 肯定会出事啊！
t_en: Of course, it won't!

t_ch: …看起来今天也排练不了了，我先走了。
t_en: ...Looks like rehearsal's over for today. I'm leaving.

t_ch: 住手啊！伊俄，你是否清醒？
t_en: Stop! Eio, do you know what you're doing?

t_ch: 不要叫我伊俄，我是埃德加！我要向不公平的命运作出反抗，我要弑神！
t_en: Don't call me Eio. I'm Edgar! I'll defy unfair fate and slay the gods!

t_ch: 你找错神了，刻法勒在另一边！
t_en: You're chasing the wrong god. Kephale is on the other side!

t_ch: 刻法勒？刻法勒从来没有给我残酷的命运。
t_en: Kephale? Kephale has never given me a cruel fate.

t_ch: 这又是什么泰坦，我没见过…
t_en: Another Titan? I haven't seen this one before...

t_ch: 我不是泰坦，我怎么可能是泰坦！
t_en: I am no Titan! How could I be a Titan!?

t_ch: 闭嘴！满口谎言的伪神，我早已受够了命运的摆弄！
t_en: Shut up! You deceitful false god, I'm done with fate's manipulation!

t_ch: 你夺走了我的战友，你夺走了我的母亲，还有妻子，你好狠的心！
t_en: You took my comrades! You took my mother! You even took my wife! You heartless monster!

t_ch: 去死吧——泰坦——
t_en: Die— Titan!

t_ch: 救命啊——！
t_en: H—Help...!

t_ch: 说归说，闹归闹，别拿生命开玩笑。
t_en: Say and do what you will, but don't play games with lives.

t_ch: 说时迟，那时快。路过的圣城卫士达利安，与你一同阻止了伊俄。
t_en: In the nick of time, passing Holy City Guard Dorian helped you stop Eio.

t_ch: 冷静了？
t_en: Have you calmed down now?

t_ch: 冷静了。
t_en: I have.

t_ch: 你们俩，认识？
t_en: You two... know each other?

t_ch: 认识。我是剧作家，他是演员。按理来说，他正在排练《被缚的埃德加》……
t_en: Yes. I'm a playwright, and he's an actor. By right, he's rehearsing The Shackled Edgar...

t_ch: 我是《被缚的埃德加》的主演伊俄，剧中扮演的就是埃德加。
t_en: I'm the lead actor, Eio, playing Edgar in The Shackled Edgar.

t_ch: 所以，刚才，是在干什么？演戏？
t_en: So, just now — was that acting?

t_ch: 怎么可能是演戏？我刚才差点被这混蛋掐到断气。喂，伊俄，你得给我个说法！
t_en: How could it be acting? That lunatic nearly strangled me! Hey, Eio, explain yourself!

t_ch: 我只是太入戏了…太过于投入了，你懂吧？就是情绪上来了，收不住。
t_en: I was just too deep into the role... too immersed. You get it, right? The emotions just overwhelmed me.

t_ch: 你收不住情绪，关我什么事？我刚打算休息一会……
t_en: What does your lack of control over your emotion have to do with me? I was just about to take a break...

t_ch: 你想嘛，「我」在戏中不是被命运左右，失去了至亲血肉，对神明感到了失望，对吗？
t_en: Think about it: In the play, "I" am manipulated by fate, losing my blood relations and loved ones. Disillusioned with the gods, isn't that right?

t_ch: 没错，我是这么写的。
t_en: Yes, that's what I wrote.

t_ch: 「我」那时应该神志不清，被仇恨蒙蔽了双眼，内心扭曲，一心想要反抗神明，对吗？
t_en: By that point, "I" should be deranged, blinded by hatred, twisted inside, and intent on defying them, correct?

t_ch: 然后，「我」就要把创世神，也就是创作《被缚的埃德加》的剧作家，给杀了，对吗？
t_en: Then, "I" should kill the creator, the playwright of The Shackled Edgar, right?

t_ch: 对对对，对你个头啊。
t_en: Yes, of cour— wait a minute, as if!

t_ch: 哦——有道理啊。
t_en: Oh... Fair enough!

t_ch: 什么乱七八糟的？
t_en: What nonsense is this?

t_ch: 剧本，不就是，他的命运吗？剧作家，就是他的创世神。
t_en: A script is his fate, and the playwright is the creator.

t_ch: 从未设想的脑回路…
t_en: I've never imagined that...

t_ch: 早在伊俄还在合唱队时，我就觉得他脑袋有问题了。
t_en: I suspected Eio wasn't all there even back when he was in the choir.

t_ch: 我看过这种文艺作品…
t_en: I've seen this kind of avant-garde work before...

t_ch: 我写的这出悲剧可不是那种故事。
t_en: What I've written here is not a simple avant-garde tragedy.

t_ch: 那这件事，就此结束？
t_en: So, is that it? Case closed?

t_ch: 不行，我不能就这样结束…也不能让你这山之民大个子来主持公道。
t_en: No, I can't just let this end... nor will I let you, a towering Mountain Dweller, act as the arbiter of justice.

t_ch: …这位朋友，我看你既不认识我们，也不涉及利益相关。你古道热肠，定能在这种「未有先例之事」上赏罚分明。而你的决断，我们俩皆能信服。
t_en: ...My friend, you appear to know neither of us, nor do you have any stake in this. With your impartiality and sense of justice, you're the perfect choice to rule on this "unprecedented matter." We both agree to trust your decision.

t_ch: 我想让你担任「一日{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}」，主持审判。
t_en: I propose you serve as the One-Day {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#}, to preside over this trial.

t_ch: 我愿意接受符合{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}精神的裁判。
t_en: I'll accept a judgment in line with the spirit of {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 「一日塔兰顿」？
t_en: One-Day Talanton?

t_ch: 嗯？你不知道吗？圣城中发生纠纷时，并非所有事务都劳烦元老。双方同意时，我们会找来「一日塔兰顿」来担当仲裁，解决纠纷。
t_en: Huh? You've never heard of it? In the holy city, not every dispute requires the elders' intervention. When both parties agree, we appoint a One-Day Talanton — a neutral party to mediate and settle disputes.

t_ch: 「一日塔兰顿」必须是纠纷双方都能信服的公道之人。向{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}起誓后，双方也必须接受「一日塔兰顿」的裁决。
t_en: This arbiter must be someone trusted by both sides to be fair. Once they swear in the name of {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#}, both parties are bound to accept their verdict.

t_ch: 有意思，就让我来审判吧。
t_en: Well, this sounds intriguing. Very well, I'll serve as the judge.

t_ch: 这样，双方都能接受。
t_en: That works for both of them.

t_ch: 首先声明，我不打白工的。
t_en: But first, let me be clear — I don't work for free.

t_ch: 我们承诺向{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}的代言人支付合理报酬。
t_en: We promise to offer fair payment as representatives of {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 你们二位，各自准备辩词，灰发{F#女士}{M#男士}，你来裁判。我们在辩场见。
t_en: Good. Then you two, prepare your arguments. Gray-haired {F#lady}{M#gentleman}, you'll be the judge. We'll meet at the trial grounds.

t_ch: 今日，于排练场中，演员伊俄，试图谋害编剧，事件——
t_en: Today, in the rehearsal hall, actor Eio stands accused of attempting to murder the playwright. This case—

t_ch: 将由，「一日塔兰顿」{NICKNAME}公平裁决。各位，请庄严宣誓——
t_en: —will be fairly judged by One-Day Talanton, {NICKNAME}. Everyone, please commit to a solemn oath...

t_ch: 以塔兰顿之名，我发誓…
t_en: In the name of Talanton, I solemnly swear...

t_ch: 我第一次当泰坦，没经验…
t_en: First time being a Titan here, kind of new to this...

t_ch: （总之，假装对上嘴型吧…）
t_en: (Just mouth along...)

t_ch: 以塔兰顿之名，我们在此庄严宣誓——我们将手持天平与宝剑，接受众神和众人的监督，不欺骗，不隐瞒，维护律法和秩序。
t_en: In the name of Talanton, we solemnly swear: We shall uphold justice with scales and sword, under the watchful eyes of gods and mortals alike. We will not deceive, we will not conceal, and we will protect the law and its order.

t_ch: 双方就位，请为自己辩护吧。
t_en: Both sides are now ready. Present your cases.

t_ch: 当时，我只是演戏太过投入了。气势汹汹的，就像是被什么附身了，这不能怪我啊。
t_en: At the time, I was simply too immersed in the performance. It was like... something possessed me! I am not at fault here.

t_ch: 别扯什么附身，不是你，还能是谁呢？{NICKNAME}大人与达利安是亲眼所见。
t_en: Don't give me that "possessed" nonsense. If it wasn't you, then who else could it be? {NICKNAME} and Dorian both saw it with their own eyes.

t_ch: 我反对！我没有伤害你的理由，但是《被缚的埃德加》的埃德加有！
t_en: I object! I had no reason to harm you, but Edgar in The Shackled Edgar certainly did!

t_ch: 是你的剧情设置让埃德加家破人亡，他对此感到不满，想要报复，有充分的犯罪动机！
t_en: It's your plot that drove Edgar's family to ruin! His resentment towards you gave him plenty of motive for revenge!

t_ch: 我反对！埃德加应该不知道创世神在哪里！
t_en: Objection! Edgar shouldn't even know where the creator resides!

t_ch: 是我告诉他的。
t_en: I told him.

t_ch: 你告诉他…我反对！再说了，《被缚的埃德加》的世界观是一个多泰坦的世界观。
t_en: You... told him? Objection! Besides, the world of The Shackled Edgar is polytheistic.

t_ch: 所以呢？
t_en: And?

t_ch: 一个神明（编剧）左右不了这个世界！
t_en: One god (playwright) can't control the whole world!

t_ch: 神明的职责各不相同，你的罪过与其他泰坦无关。
t_en: Each deity has its own responsibilities. Your sins don't concern the others.

t_ch: 可编剧根本没有什么发言权。
t_en: But playwrights don't even have much say in the script.

t_ch: 没错，你们演员也参与创作，你为什么不对自己动手呢？
t_en: True, actors do contribute to the creative process. Why not direct your fury at yourself?

t_ch: 别管我，我的脑子正在烧烤…
t_en: Leave me alone, my brain is still trying to make sense of it...

t_ch: 你，「一日塔兰顿」，应当头脑清晰。
t_en: You, One-Day Talanton, are supposed to keep a clear head.

t_ch: …我受不了蠢货了。
t_en: ...I've had enough of fools.

t_ch: *唐突地清嗓*发言快结束了，你要仔细分辨。
t_en: *Suddenly clear throat* The address is almost over, be sure to make good judgment.

t_ch: 我承认这里存在着一些悖论，但按常识来说，埃德加不会认知到剧本外的世界。
t_en: I admit there are some paradoxes here, but by common sense, Edgar wouldn't be aware of a world beyond the script.

t_ch: 只有你伊俄能认知到剧本外的世界，所以动手的就是你，伊俄！
t_en: Only you, Eio, have such awareness. Thus, you're the one who acted!

t_ch: 不，观测是取决于第三者的。
t_en: No, observation depends on the third party!

t_ch: 我，伊俄，同时认识你忒斯庇斯，和《被缚的埃德加》的埃德加。反过来说，埃德加能借助我，伊俄，认识你《被缚的埃德加》的编剧，忒斯庇斯。
t_en: I, Eio, am connected to both you, Thespis, and The Shackled Edgar. Therefore, Edgar, through me, gains awareness of his creator, Thespis.

t_ch: 我反对！你就是伊俄，你不是埃德加！你不能替埃德加发声。
t_en: Objection! You are Eio, not Edgar! You can't speak on Edgar's behalf.

t_ch: 「我是谁」这个问题一直很有争议，这是由他人决定的吗？还是自己决定自己是谁？
t_en: The question of "Who am I?" has always been debated. Is identity decided by others? Or by oneself?

t_ch: 我反对「他人决定论」，否则，你说我是猪，我就真的是猪了吗？
t_en: I reject the idea that identity is determined by others. Otherwise, if someone called me a pig, would I really be a pig?

t_ch: 换言之，人只能自己决定自己是谁。我认为我是伊俄，我也可以认为自己是埃德加。
t_en: In short, one determines their own identity. I see myself as Eio, but I can also choose to see myself as Edgar.

t_ch: …别扯那些了。你只是想说，埃德加应该接受惩罚，而伊俄无罪？
t_en: ...Enough philosophy. Are you just arguing that Edgar should be punished, while Eio is innocent?

t_ch: 正是。
t_en: Precisely.

t_ch: 可你要是真的杀了我，剧情就不存在了，埃德加也就不存在了，就只有你伊俄了！
t_en: But if you had actually killed me, the story would cease to exist, Edgar wouldn't exist, and only you, Eio, would remain!

t_ch: 真到那时，你还能如此狡辩吗？
t_en: Could you still twist logic then?

t_ch: 幸好我没有做到。
t_en: Fortunately, it didn't come to that.

t_ch: 尊敬的，「一日塔兰顿」，{NICKNAME}大人，公正的裁断，请做出。
t_en: Honorable One-Day Talanton, {M#Sir}{F#Madam} {NICKNAME}, please deliver your just verdict.

t_ch: 伊俄，你负全责。
t_en: Eio, the full responsibility is yours.

t_ch: 我，我明白了，我接受全责。
t_en: I understand. I accept full responsibility.

t_ch: 伊俄，你和埃德加各一半责任。
t_en: Eio, the responsibility is shared — half yours, half Edgar's.

t_ch: 从犯之责，我明白了，我接受。
t_en: As an accomplice. I understand. I accept.

t_ch: 忒斯庇斯，你负全责。
t_en: Thespis, the full responsibility is yours.

t_ch: 是我挨了一顿打，我还要负全责吗？我以为我们在讨论对伊俄惩罚的轻重……
t_en: What? I took the beating, and now I'm to blame? I thought we were discussing how to punish Eio, not me...

t_ch: 都怪你写得太烂了。
t_en: It's your fault for writing something so terrible.

t_ch: 都怪你写得太好了。
t_en: It's your fault for writing something too brilliant.

t_ch: 你们俩各打五十大板。
t_en: Both of you deserve fifty lashes.

t_ch: 好吧，谁让我也宣誓了呢。
t_en: Well, I did swear an oath, so... fair enough.

t_ch: 感谢您的公平裁决，「一日塔兰顿」{NICKNAME}大人。
t_en: Thank you for your fair judgment, Honorable One-Day Talanton, {M#Sir}{F#Madam} {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 一场小小闹剧就此结束，围观人群散去…你注意到「神礼观众」似乎意犹未尽。
t_en: The commotion ended, and the crowd dispersed... but you notice the Theoros seems far from satisfied.

t_ch: 「一日塔兰顿」阁下，真是有趣的裁判。
t_en: {M#Sir}{F#Madam} One-Day Talanton, an intriguing verdict indeed.

t_ch: 我听说，曾有一名铁匠日夜钻研雕刻技艺，有一天他梦见了一位完美的少女，于是他用象牙雕刻出了一座栩栩如生的少女雕像。
t_en: I once heard of a blacksmith who devoted his life to perfecting the art of sculpture. One night, he dreamed of a flawless maiden. Inspired, he carved a lifelike ivory statue of her.

t_ch: 后来{RUBY_B#浪漫之泰坦}墨涅塔{RUBY_E#}垂怜他，神明用奇迹为那座雕像赋予了生命，并让他们二人结为夫妻，过上了幸福的生活。
t_en: Moved by his passion, {RUBY_B#Romance Titan}Mnestia{RUBY_E#} blessed the statue with life, and they became a happy couple.

t_ch: 我好像听说过这样的故事。
t_en: I've heard a story like that before.

t_ch: 所以，为什么说起故事来？
t_en: So why are we talking about stories now?

t_ch: 呵呵，我更喜欢这种创造者与造物和谐共存的故事。
t_en: Heh, I just prefer tales where creators and creations live in harmony.

t_ch: 创造者若投来的是爱，造物便会回应爱。若创造者投来的是遗忘，造物便会回应遗忘。
t_en: When a creator offers love, their creation responds with love. But if the creator casts them aside, the creation answers with oblivion.

t_ch: …时间不早了，在下先告辞了。
t_en: ...It's late. I should take my leave.

t_ch: 神明啊，你都做了什么！
t_en: Gods, what have you done!?

t_ch: 我的爱人，你快醒醒。
t_en: My love, please wake up.

t_ch: 多么可怜的命运啊！
t_en: What a pitiful fate!

t_ch: 埃德加，你清醒一些…
t_en: Edgar, come to your senses...

t_ch: （什么情况？追上去看看吧。）
t_en: (What's happening? Follow and find out.)

t_ch: （「一日塔兰顿」…不就是公益法官吗？帮忙就帮到底吧……）
t_en: ("One-Day Talanton"... Isn't that just a fancy name for a public interest judge? Guess I'm in it for the long haul...)

t_ch: （…怎么会有这么多人？）
t_en: (...Why are there so many people here?)

t_ch: （虽然过程莫名其妙的…至少「一日塔兰顿」的奖励还不赖。）
t_en: (At least the reward for this odd role isn't half bad.)

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}，又见面啦。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, we meet again.

t_ch: 又见面了，小赛飞儿。
t_en: We meet again, little Cipher.

t_ch: 赛飞儿…那是谁？
t_en: Cipher... Who's that?

t_ch: 啊，和{F#女士}{M#先生}您报一报喜，我已经不会再小偷小摸啦，现在也不用缩在箱子里睡觉了。
t_en: Ah, {F#miss}{M#mister}, I wanted to share some good news — I'm not stealing petty things anymore, and I don't have to curl up in a box to sleep now.

t_ch: 云石市集的大家很照顾我，我现在过得很开心，只是……
t_en: Everyone at the Marmoreal Market has looked after me. I'm quite happy nowadays, but...

t_ch: 你是哪位来着？
t_en: Who are you again?

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}不记得我了吗…我叫希瑞雅，之前偷拿过你的钱袋。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, you don't remember me? I'm Serena, and I once stole your pouch of coins.

t_ch: 自从上次的事以后，大家的帮助让我很开心，我也在努力改变自己，只是……
t_en: Since that incident, I've been so happy with everyone's help, and I'm striving to change myself. But...

t_ch: 你在这里做什么？
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: 我听说，在比云石天宫还要高的山上，有很多人聚在一起，决定了奥赫玛的未来。
t_en: I heard that on a mountain even higher than the Marmoreal Palace, many people have gathered to decide Okhema's future.

t_ch: 市集的大家都在谈论这件事。虽然大家很信任阿格莱雅大人，但也有不好的传言。我感到害怕……
t_en: Everyone in the market is talking about it. Though they trust Lady Aglaea, there are also ominous rumors. It scares me...

t_ch: 奥赫玛，会不会也像阿卡索斯那样，变成一个黑漆漆、乱糟糟，可怕怪物遍地走的地方？
t_en: Will Okhema become like Icatus — dark, chaotic, full of terrifying monsters everywhere?

t_ch: 所以，我想去拜访一下伟大的刻法勒。据说正是因为它的庇佑，圣城才能挺立至今…也许只要和它说说话，我就不害怕了。
t_en: That's why I want to visit the great Kephale. They say the holy city stands to this day thanks to their protection... maybe if I talk to them, I won't be afraid.

t_ch: 但守卫大叔不让我进去……
t_en: But the guard won't let me in...

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}，你是奥赫玛的大英雄，肯定能进出云崖。你可以带我去刻法勒的身边吗？
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, you're Okhema's great hero, so you must be able to come and go at Dawncloud. Could you bring me to Kephale?

t_ch: 还是守规矩吧，别去了。
t_en: Rules are made to be followed. Let's not go.

t_ch: 嗯…我就不添麻烦啦，但我想再待一会儿，说不定有其他办法能进去……
t_en: Mmm... I won't be a bother, but I want to wait a little longer. Maybe there's another way in...

t_ch: 包在我身上！
t_en: Leave it to me!

t_ch: 真的吗？谢谢你！
t_en: Really? Thank you!

t_ch: 那个，可以拜托你去和守卫大叔沟通一下吗，他看上去有些凶，我不敢过去……
t_en: Um, would you mind talking to the guard? He looks kinda scary, and I'm too scared to approach...

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}，你是改变心意了吗？
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, did you change your mind?

t_ch: 还是守规矩吧，别去了。
t_en: Rules are made to be followed. Let's not go.

t_ch: 嗯…我就不添麻烦啦，但我想再待一会儿，说不定有其他办法能进去……
t_en: Mmm... I won't be a bother, but I want to wait a little longer. Maybe there's another way in...

t_ch: 包在我身上！
t_en: Leave it to me!

t_ch: 真的吗？谢谢你！
t_en: Really? Thank you!

t_ch: 那个，可以拜托你去和守卫大叔沟通一下吗，他看上去有些凶，我不敢过去……
t_en: Um, would you mind talking to the guard? He looks kinda scary, and I'm too scared to approach...

t_ch: 你好，{NICKNAME}贵宾，请问有什么能帮到您？
t_en: Good day, distinguished guest, {NICKNAME}. How may I help you?

t_ch: 你，听说过我的故事？
t_en: You— have you heard my story?

t_ch: 当然，您是元老院专门嘱咐过、记录在册的贵宾。听说您和其他黄金裔一同击败了尼卡多利，我哪敢怠慢呢。
t_en: Of course. You're a VIP the Council of Elders specifically mentioned and recorded. I heard you and the other Chrysos Heirs defeated Nikador — there's no way I'd slack off around you.

t_ch: 帮我个忙，我想带个人进去。
t_en: Do me a favor: I want to bring someone in.

t_ch: 我想带个人进去。
t_en: I want to bring someone in.

t_ch: 贵宾，您说的是那个小女孩吗？
t_en: Distinguished guest, are you referring to that small girl?

t_ch: 这…恕难从命，我们也有职责在身，不能随意给平民放行，还请贵宾理解一二……
t_en: This... my apologies, but we have our duties. We can't casually allow civilians through. I hope you understand...

t_ch: 如实相告。
t_en: Tell him the truth.

t_ch: 试着用诡计迷惑他。
t_en: Try confusing him with a trick.

t_ch: 其实，那个小女孩可不是平民……
t_en: Actually, that small girl isn't just some ordinary civilian...

t_ch: 她是凯妮斯的孙女。
t_en: She's the granddaughter of Caenis.

t_ch: 她是元老院凯妮斯的孙女！
t_en: She's the granddaughter of Elder Caenis!

t_ch: 凯妮斯大人的孙女？嗯…硬要说的话，她看上去确实和那位大人有些相像……
t_en: The granddaughter of Lord Caenis? Hmm...if you insist, she does look a bit like that esteemed figure...

t_ch: 可要是你说的属实，她的名字和相貌应该早就记录在贵宾册里了。
t_en: But if what you're saying is true, her name and appearance should already be recorded in the VIP register.

t_ch: 骗不到他，得想想别的办法……
t_en: We can't fool him. We need another approach...

t_ch: 她是异邦的黄金裔。
t_en: She's a Chrysos Heir from the outlands.

t_ch: 她是来自阿卡索斯的黄金裔！
t_en: She's a Chrysos Heir from Icatus!

t_ch: 阿卡索斯？印象里是早已覆灭的遥远城邦吧。
t_en: Icatus? If memory serves, that's a distant city-state long since fallen.

t_ch: 对不起，贵宾，就算是异邦的黄金裔，也得经元老院认可方能进入。
t_en: I'm sorry, honored guest. Even if she's a foreign Chrysos Heir, she needs the Council of Elders' approval to enter.

t_ch: 骗不到他，得想想别的办法……
t_en: We can't fool him. We need another approach...

t_ch: 她是飞贼赛飞儿。
t_en: She's the phantom thief, Cipher.

t_ch: 她是继承扎格列斯之力的半神，跑得飞快的神偷，黄金裔赛飞儿！
t_en: She's Cipher, a demigod who inherited Zagreus's power, a lightning-fast master thief and Chrysos Heir!

t_ch: 赛飞儿，这个名字我有印象，确实是背负着神权的黄金裔，她的画像也有记录在册，容我看看。
t_en: Cipher... I do recall that name. She's indeed a Chrysos Heir endowed with divine authority. Her portrait should be on file — let me check.

t_ch: 守卫打开随身携带的贵宾册，找到赛飞儿的画像，又看了看几步开外的希瑞雅，皱起眉头。
t_en: The guard opens his VIP register, finds Cipher's portrait, then glances at Serena a few steps away and frowns.

t_ch: …这画像是她吗？
t_en: ...Is this portrait really her?

t_ch: 是，这会儿她才八岁。
t_en: Yes, she's only eight right now.

t_ch: 是，那时她还不够瘦。
t_en: Right. She wasn't that slim in those days.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}贵宾，您就别消遣我了……
t_en: Honored guest {NICKNAME}, please don't toy with me...

t_ch: 还是说实话吧。
t_en: Better to tell the truth.

t_ch: 你将希瑞雅的愿望如实讲给了守卫。他看上去有些动容。
t_en: You tell the guard about Serena's wish honestly. He seems somewhat moved.

t_ch: 我明白了…但是贵宾，你要知道，刻法勒已经陷入长眠，许久不曾给出回应了。
t_en: I see... But, honored guest, you should know that Kephale has long been in a deep slumber, and hasn't responded in ages.

t_ch: 而且不管怎么说，规矩就是规矩。哪怕看在贵宾你的面子上放她进去，这里人多眼杂，难免会被发现……
t_en: Also, rules are rules. Even if I let her in out of respect for you, there are so many people around that someone would surely notice...

t_ch: 不如这样，贵宾：明天的门扉时，你再带这个小女孩过来。那会儿人少，只要你快去快回就不会出问题…但愿吧。
t_en: Let's do this, honored guest: Come back tomorrow at the Entry Hour with the girl. Fewer people will be around then. As long as you're quick, there shouldn't be any trouble... hopefully.

t_ch: 你将守卫的建议告诉希瑞雅，并与她约定明天门扉时碰头。
t_en: You pass the guard's suggestion on to Serena, and agree to meet her tomorrow at the Entry Hour.

t_ch: 到了第二天清晨……
t_en: The next morning...

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}，真是麻烦你了，要陪我起个大早…守卫大叔说得没错，这会儿人确实少了许多，幸好跟他打过招呼了。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, sorry for making you get up so early... The guard was right, there are definitely fewer people now. Good thing we talked to him ahead of time.

t_ch: 不过他今早不在，也没见其他人把我们拦下来…{F#女士}{M#先生}，我们该往哪走？
t_en: But he's not here this morning, and nobody else stopped us... {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, where should we go from here?

t_ch: 女孩打量着四周的壮景，眼里充满了好奇。你突然意识到，今天过后，她可能再也没有机会拜访黎明云崖了。
t_en: The girl takes in the grand scenery with curious eyes. You suddenly realize that after today, she might never get another chance to visit Dawncloud.

t_ch: 机会难得，我带你稍微逛逛吧。
t_en: This is a rare opportunity. Let me show you around a bit.

t_ch: 好呀！我不认得路，要麻烦{F#女士}{M#先生}你带路啦。
t_en: Great! I don't know the way, so please lead on, {F#miss}{M#mister}.

t_ch: 哇，好壮观的壁画…比刻法勒广场的那幅还要大！
t_en: Wow, what a spectacular mural... it's even bigger than the one at Kephale Plaza!

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}，这上面讲的是什么呀？
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, what's the story behind this mural?

t_ch: 小火盆烤大眼萌。
t_en: A small brazier roasting big-eyed cuties.

t_ch: 威吓特性生效了。
t_en: The intimidation trait took effect.

t_ch: 听不大懂呢…但是这个大眼睛指的应该是艾格勒吧？我这些日子读了许多泰坦的故事，勉强认得出来。
t_en: That's kind of hard to grasp... but that big eye must be Aquila, right? I've read a bunch of Titan stories lately, so I can sort of recognize it.

t_ch: 感觉艾格勒的光特别亮、特别烫，人们都很害怕的样子……
t_en: Aquila's light feels so dazzling and scorching, it looks like everyone's really afraid...

t_ch: 这张壁画描绘的是…刻法勒接过黎明机器的场景？我好像在克帖西芬姐姐的书画集里看到过……
t_en: This mural depicts... Kephale receiving the "Dawn Device"? I think I saw that in Miss Ctesiphona's art collection...

t_ch: 据说，黎明机器是艾格勒赠送给刻法勒的礼物，艾格勒把许多光芒都塞进了这颗巨大的机械球里。
t_en: Supposedly, the Dawn Device was a gift from Aquila to Kephale — Aquila stuffed lots of radiance into that huge mechanical sphere.

t_ch: 在艾格勒闭上所有眼睛、令世界坠入黑暗之后，刻法勒从自己的王座上起身，为世人背负起最后的黎明……
t_en: After Aquila closed all their eyes and plunged the world into darkness, Kephale rose from their throne to shoulder the world's final dawn...

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}，你发现了吗：刚才那幅壁画里，许多人跪在地上表示臣服；而这幅壁画里，人们却自然地伸出手分享光明。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, did you notice? In the previous mural, lots of people knelt down in submission, but in this one, everyone naturally reaches out to share the light.

t_ch: 也许这暗示了两任烈阳之间的差别——艾格勒的光照曾经耀眼无情，而如今刻法勒的黎明却更加温暖柔和……
t_en: Maybe it's hinting at the difference between the two suns — Aquila's light was once harsh and merciless, while Kephale's dawn seems warmer and gentler...

t_ch: 不是，你真会啊？
t_en: No way... Are you for real?

t_ch: 谢谢你的夸奖！要是过会儿，刻法勒真的能回应我就好啦……
t_en: Thanks for the compliment! I really hope Kephale will respond to me soon...

t_ch: 哇，天生祭司圣体！
t_en: Wow, a natural-born talent for priesthood!

t_ch: 谢谢你的夸奖！要是过会儿，刻法勒真的能回应我就好啦……
t_en: Thanks for the compliment! I really hope Kephale will respond to me soon...

t_ch: 呃，你可能有点过度解读…
t_en: Uh, you might be reading too much into it...

t_ch: 啊，对哦，谦逊也是重要的美德呢！要是过会儿，刻法勒能亲自告诉我一些答案就好啦……
t_en: Ah, you're right — humility is an important virtue, too! If only Kephale could personally give me some answers soon...

t_ch: 好漂亮的广场…周围能看到好多泰坦的符号，我都还认不全呢。
t_en: What a beautiful plaza... I see so many Titan symbols all around, and I don't recognize them all yet.

t_ch: 刻法勒也离我们好近…我们在这里说话，它能听到吗？
t_en: Kephale's so close to us... If we talk here, can they hear us?

t_ch: 呵，连如何向刻法勒行礼都不明白的稚童，还妄想让泰坦回应叨扰？
t_en: Heh, a clueless child who doesn't even know how to pay respects to Kephale, yet still thinks a Titan will answer her trivial chatter?

t_ch: 小女娃，我问你，你知道什么是刻法勒祭仪吗？
t_en: Young girl, let me ask: Do you know what the Kephale ritual is?

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}，和刻法勒说话需要学习什么复杂的仪式吗？我从没有听说过……
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, do I really need to learn these complicated rituals to talk with Kephale? I've never heard that before...

t_ch: 呵，小女娃，你尽可以向我请教，别被邪魔外道带歪了信仰。
t_en: Heh, little girl, you're free to ask me anything. Don't let these heretical outsiders lead your faith astray.

t_ch: 这么说，您很懂刻法勒？
t_en: So you know Kephale well?

t_ch: 信仰心可没有贵贱之分。
t_en: Nobility matters not when it comes to faith.

t_ch: 可笑，我在这里主持了几十年的祭仪，没人比我更懂刻法勒。
t_en: Ridiculous! I've presided over the rituals for decades — no one knows Kephale better than I do.

t_ch: 老爷爷，市集的大家才不是邪魔外道！大家都跟我说，只要诚心和刻法勒对话，它就一定能听到！
t_en: Grandpa, the people at the market aren't heretics! Everyone says as long as we speak earnestly to Kephale, they'll definitely hear us!

t_ch: 可悲，如此稚童，竟已染上了顽固的恶习。正好，时候还早，我就以刻法勒大祭司的身份来矫正后辈吧。
t_en: How sad, that such a young child has already picked up these stubborn bad habits. Fine, since it's still early, I, the high priest of Kephale, shall enlighten the next generation.

t_ch: 以大欺小，真不害臊。
t_en: Bullying someone so much younger... have you no shame?

t_ch: 倚老卖老，令人发笑。
t_en: Throwing your age around — pathetic.

t_ch: 前辈，请用力矫正我们！
t_en: Old-timer, go ahead — straighten us out with all your might!

t_ch: 呵，我可不会被你这样的小辈激怒。信仰一事，本就无关年纪大小。
t_en: Heh, someone like you can't provoke me. Faith has nothing to do with age.

t_ch: 小女娃，我考你一考：刻法勒的祭仪有三个流程，怎样做的？
t_en: Little girl, let me test you: The Kephale ritual has three steps. How is it performed?

t_ch: 我不知道。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 将供奉放在祭坛，双手合十，诚心诵念三遍祷词。
t_en: Place the offering on the altar, fold your hands, and recite the prayer three times with sincerity.

t_ch: 你推崇的所谓平民祭法，连有价值的供奉都不舍得拿出来，刻法勒怎么可能给予回应？
t_en: This "civilian's rite" you favor doesn't even provide a valuable offering. Why would Kephale respond to that?

t_ch: 可是，大家平常都不容易，如果还要花钱买供奉……
t_en: But everyone already struggles day to day — now they'd have to spend money on an offering...?

t_ch: 小女娃，那不过是他们为自己的吝啬狡辩罢了。供奉乃是牺牲，没有牺牲还奢谈什么信仰？
t_en: Girl, that's just their excuse for being stingy. An offering is a sacrifice — how can you call it faith with no sacrifice?

t_ch: 老爷爷，我想请问，刻法勒和我们…究竟是什么关系呢？
t_en: Grandpa, may I ask what exactly the relationship between Kephale and us is?

t_ch: 哈哈哈！居然有人问我刻法勒和我们是什么关系？
t_en: Hahaha! Someone's actually asking me what Kephale has to do with us?

t_ch: 看呐，它高居云崖之巅，向奥赫玛的每一个人投去注视。我们的一切都因它的注视才得以存在。如此尊神，怎能不拜，怎能不为它献上牺牲？
t_en: Look, they sit atop Dawncloud, gazing upon every soul in Okhema. All that we are exists because of their watchful eye. A god so revered — how could we not worship and sacrifice to them?

t_ch: 可是，翁法罗斯的每一位吟游诗人都会说，刻法勒是全知的父、是众生的守护神。
t_en: But every bard in Amphoreus says Kephale is the all-knowing father, the guardian deity of all beings.

t_ch: 我不明白全知的父意味着什么…但是，它也像所有真正的爸爸一样，不会向自己的孩子索要东西。
t_en: I don't quite know what "all-knowing father" means... but like every real dad, they wouldn't demand anything from their children.

t_ch: 我听说，爸爸为了保护我离开阿卡索斯，高举着一块门板、在人群的后面拦着可怕的怪物。
t_en: I heard that my dad left Icatus to protect me, holding up a door plank behind the crowd to block terrifying monsters.

t_ch: 现在的刻法勒…不也正是为了它的造物们免遭黑暗，才高举黎明机器的吗？
t_en: Isn't Kephale doing the same now — raising the Dawn Device to keep their creations safe from darkness?

t_ch: 我不知道拜见尊神应该遵循何种礼仪。我只知道，见到爸爸，只要真诚地说话…就一定能得到温柔的抚慰。
t_en: I don't know what rituals you follow when meeting a god. I just know that if I speak sincerely to my dad... I'll always receive his gentle comfort.

t_ch: <size=28>爸爸才不会对自己的孩子冷眼相看！</size>
t_en: <size=28>A father would never greet his children with a cold shoulder!</size>

t_ch: 希瑞雅抽噎着跑开了。
t_en: Serena sobs and runs off.

t_ch: 呵，你以为我会被稚童的胡言乱语扭曲信仰么？祭仪该有的步骤，我一样都不会少做！
t_en: Heh, you think a child's naive rambling will warp my faith? I'll go through every proper step of the ritual!

t_ch: 刻法勒和你们是什么关系？
t_en: What's Kephale to you people?

t_ch: 刻法勒…当然是众生的父亲了。
t_en: Kephale... obviously the father of all living beings.

t_ch: …告辞。
t_en: ...Farewell.

t_ch: 对不起，{F#女士}{M#先生}…刚才有点没忍住。
t_en: Sorry, {F#Miss}{M#Mister}... I lost my composure just now.

t_ch: 不过我活这么大，早就学会了如何平复心情。现在的我已经完全振作起来了！
t_en: But I've been around long enough to know how to calm myself. I'm totally fine now!

t_ch: 很好，很有精神！
t_en: Great, you're in good spirits!

t_ch: 你才活了多大岁数…
t_en: How old are you, really...?

t_ch: 嘿嘿…我可觉得自己已经是个大人了呢~
t_en: Hehe... I think I'm already an adult!

t_ch: 不怪你，怪刚才那个老头。
t_en: It's not your fault — blame that old man from before.

t_ch: …等下次见到那个老爷爷，还是和他道个歉吧。他也只是活在自己的世界里……
t_en: ...Next time I see that old man, I should still apologize to him. He's just living in his own world...

t_ch: 抬头就能看到刻法勒…虽然很高、很冷，但一伸手就能摸到黎明机器的光芒。
t_en: If you just look up, you can see Kephale... They're so tall and cold, but if you stretch out your hand, you can touch the light of the Dawn Device.

t_ch: 在这里说话…刻法勒一定能听见了吧。
t_en: If we speak here... surely Kephale can hear us now, right?

t_ch: 伟大的刻法勒啊，背负那么大的黎明机器，你一定很累吧……
t_en: O mighty Kephale, bearing that enormous Dawn Device — surely you must be exhausted...

t_ch: 可是，我想恳求您，千万不要把奥赫玛的光明丢下……
t_en: But I beg of you, please don't abandon the light of Okhema...

t_ch: 希瑞雅大声地祈祷着。知晓泰坦真相的你很清楚，刻法勒并不会真的回应她。
t_en: Serena prays aloud. You, knowing the Titan's truth, are well aware that Kephale won't truly answer her.

t_ch: 直到你听见一个浑厚低沉的嗓音传来……
t_en: ...Until you hear a deep, resonant voice approaching...

t_ch: 孩子，不要害怕——
t_en: Child, do not fret.

t_ch: 啊…这声音，是刻法勒吗？
t_en: Ah... this sound, are you Kephale?

t_ch: 是我，全世之座，众生之父，永昼的背负者。
t_en: It is I, the Throne of Worlds, Father of All Beings, and Bearer of Eternal Day.

t_ch: 孩子啊，不要害怕——我会为你们背负这沉重的黎明，将漆黑的凶潮隔绝于圣城之外——直到永远。
t_en: Child, fear not — I will carry the burden of this heavy dawn, shielding the Holy City from the tides of darkness... for all eternity.

t_ch: 太好了，刻法勒大人！…请再多陪我一会儿吧，我还有好多好多话想说给你听……
t_en: That'd be wonderful! Great Kephale... Please stay with me a little longer. I still have so much to tell you...

t_ch: 事有蹊跷。你从没见过这么不「谜语人」的泰坦，而且……
t_en: Something's off. You've never met such a straightforward Titan before, and...

t_ch: 声源不应该那么近。
t_en: The voice is coming from way too close.

t_ch: 果然，不出你所料，说话的不是刻法勒——而是那名守卫。
t_en: Just as you suspected, it's not Kephale speaking — it's the guard.

t_ch: 他拿着一枚石制扩声器，压低声音，努力扮出一位泰坦该有的威严和仁慈。
t_en: He's holding a voice amplifier made from stone, lowering his voice to imitate the majesty and compassion of a Titan.

t_ch: 呜呜，我知道爸爸妈妈已经回不来了…我会努力长大，成为一个坚强的人…但实在坚强不起来的时候，可以拜托刻法勒大人听我倾诉吗？
t_en: *sob* I know Mom and Dad aren't coming back... I'll try to grow up strong... But when I just can't be strong... Can I confide in you, great Kephale?

t_ch: 当然，刻法勒会平等地聆听每一位子民的祈求。
t_en: Of course. Kephale would listen to people's prayers equally.

t_ch: 这种时候…还是不要打搅为好呢。
t_en: At times like this... maybe it's best not to interrupt.

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}，久等啦。刻法勒大人真的很好，耐心地听我说了好多话。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, thanks for waiting! The great Kephale was really kind and patiently listened to everything I had to say.

t_ch: 现在我心里暖洋洋的，一点也不害怕了！
t_en: Now, my heart feels all warm and fuzzy. I'm not scared at all!

t_ch: 从今天起，你也是小太阳！
t_en: From today onward, you're a little ray of sunshine!

t_ch: 嘿嘿~那我要把今天从刻法勒大人那儿感受的温暖带回市集，与大家一同分享！
t_en: Hehe~ Then I'll take the warmth I felt from the great Kephale today back to the market and share it with everyone!

t_ch: 好羡慕你，能和泰坦说话。
t_en: I'm so jealous you got to speak to a Titan.

t_ch: 没关系，{F#女士}{M#先生}，像你这样的大英雄，一定有机会和它说上话的。说不定到时候，刻法勒大人反而会紧张呢！
t_en: It's okay, {F#miss}{M#mister}. Someone as heroic as you will definitely get a chance to speak to them someday. Who knows. Maybe the great Kephale will be the one getting nervous then!

t_ch: 谢谢你一路陪我爬到这里…时候不早啦，{F#女士}{M#先生}，我们一起回去吧！
t_en: Thank you for climbing all the way up here with me... It's getting late, {F#miss}{M#mister}. Let's head back together!

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}，又见面啦！谢谢你带我去见刻法勒！
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, we meet again! Thanks for taking me to see Kephale!

t_ch: 市集的朋友们听说我的经历之后，都露出了特别欣慰的笑容。刻法勒大人一定也保佑了他们吧。
t_en: When my friends at the market heard about what I had been through, they all smiled so warmly. I bet Kephale blessed them, too.

t_ch: 现在，大家都对奥赫玛的未来更有希望啦！
t_en: Now, everyone feels more hopeful about the future of Okhema!

t_ch: 啊，是你啊，{NICKNAME}贵宾。有什么能帮到你？
t_en: Oh, it's you, {NICKNAME}. Anything I can help you with?

t_ch: 兄弟，演戏天赋不错。
t_en: Gotta hand it to you, buddy — your acting skills are top-notch.

t_ch: 嚯，这刻法勒可真年轻。
t_en: Huh, who knew Kephale was this young?

t_ch: 我想当一日刻法勒，能安排吗？
t_en: I wanna be Kephale for a day. Think you can make it happen?

t_ch: 那姑娘总是能碰上好心人…
t_en: That kid's luck is something else... always meeting kind people.

t_ch: 哈哈，果然被贵宾发现了。这件事还请你替我保密…善意的谎言，有时候也很有必要。
t_en: Haha, you caught me. Please keep it a secret... Sometimes, a little white lie is just what's needed.

t_ch: 听你这么说，你很懂哦。
t_en: Huh, you really get it, don't you?

t_ch: 你这么做，有什么理由吗？
t_en: So... why'd you do it?

t_ch: 我小的时候，奥赫玛可不像现在这样太平，三天两头都有纷争眷属袭击城墙。前一天还见过的人，可能第二天就……
t_en: When I was little, Okhema wasn't as peaceful as it is now. Attacks on the city walls by Strife Titankins were almost a daily thing. One day you'd see someone, and the next...

t_ch: 我父亲是一名圣城守卫。每当战事吃紧，他就会拿上头盔和长矛，一整宿都不回来。而我只能独自留在家中，把自己埋进被子里，试图隔绝窗外纷争眷属的尖啸声…
t_en: My father was a guard of the holy city. When battles were fierce, he'd grab his helmet and spear and wouldn't come back all night. And I'd be left alone at home, burying myself under the blankets to drown out the screeching of the Strife Titankins outside...

t_ch: 在某个安静的幕匿时，我对着窗外的刻法勒神体祈祷，突然听到了它的回应。
t_en: One quiet Curtain-Fall Hour, as I prayed to the statue of Kephale outside the window... I heard them respond.

t_ch: 它说：我是刻法勒，全世之座、众生之父，永昼的背负者。孩子啊，不要害怕……
t_en: They said: I am Kephale, the Throne of Worlds, Father of All Beings, Bearer of Eternal Day. Child, fear not...

t_ch: 刻法勒的宽慰给了我勇气，让我得以在纷争的尖啸中入睡。直到某天，我照例向刻法勒祈祷，却没有得到回应……
t_en: Kephale's comfort gave me courage. It helped me sleep amidst the chaos. But one day, when I prayed to Kephale as usual... there was no answer.

t_ch: 第二天清晨，我才得知，父亲在昨日的圣城保卫战中牺牲了。
t_en: The next morning, I found out... my father had fallen in battle defending the holy city.

t_ch: 其他守卫把这枚石制的扩声器交给了我。这是在父亲的身上发现的，是他的号角、他的乐器……
t_en: The other guards handed me this stone voice amplifier. They found it on my father... it was his horn, his instrument...

t_ch: 也是他以刻法勒之名，用另一种方式陪伴孩子的证明。
t_en: It was proof that, in the name of Kephale, he stayed by his child's side in his own way.

t_ch: 贵宾，你说…刻法勒真的能听到我们说话吗？
t_en: Dear guest... Do you think Kephale can really hear our prayers?

t_ch: 伟大的天父刻法勒…
t_en: Oh, the great sky father, Kephale...

t_ch: 您会驱散我的恐惧吗？
t_en: Will you drive away my fears?

t_ch: 可惜我没法去见您…
t_en: Too bad that I can't meet you in person...

t_ch: 黎明云崖，有什么值得一看的景色呢……
t_en: Is there anything worth seeing at Dawncloud...

t_ch: 抓紧时间，沿路带她多走走吧。
t_en: Hurry up, and take her for a walk along the way.

t_ch: 这边…真的能上去吗？
t_en: Can we really go up here?

t_ch: 啊，离刻法勒越来越近了……
t_en: Ah, we're getting closer to Kephale...

t_ch: 好大的湖面呀！它会不会曾经是刻法勒大人的镜子…或者洗脸池？
t_en: What a huge lake! Could it have been the great Kephale's mirror... or maybe their washbasin?

t_ch: 「长长的直阶，通往全世的座前。」——原来画本里说的都是真的！
t_en: "The long straight stairway leads to the Throne of Worlds" — So the picture book was telling the truth!

t_ch: 究竟是为什么呢？难道…是怕我抢走他的风头？
t_en: Why is that, exactly? Could it be... that he's afraid I'll steal his spotlight?

t_ch: ——哦，是你啊，我的崇拜者。抱歉，今天不能给你签名。
t_en: —Oh, it's you, my admirer. Sorry, I can't give you an autograph today.

t_ch: 你在嘟囔什么呢？
t_en: What are you mumbling about?

t_ch: 可恶，没得签了吗？
t_en: Darn it, no autograph this time?

t_ch: 今天我的确没有签名的兴致。
t_en: I'm really not in the mood to sign autographs today.

t_ch: 谁想要签名啊？
t_en: Who even wants your autograph?

t_ch: 哦？面对我这个「竞技场英雄」亲笔签名的诱惑也无动于衷吗？你这家伙，有点胆识。
t_en: Oh? Completely unfazed by the temptation of a personal autograph from the "Arena Hero"? You've got some guts, I'll give you that.

t_ch: 不过话说回来，今天我的确没有签名的兴致。
t_en: But that being said, I'm really not in the mood to sign autographs today.

t_ch: 黑潮肆虐，不少人还被困在各处，在危险中呼唤着我的名字！作为蝉联三届「奥凯纳斯运动会」掷铁饼冠军的竞技豪雄，我怎么能视若无睹？
t_en: The black tide is raging, and many people are still trapped in various places, calling my name in distress! As a three-time consecutive "Okeanas Games" throwing champion and a true warrior of competition, how could I stand idly by?

t_ch: 可是克拉特鲁斯那老顽固，吩咐卫兵不准任何人外出——尤其是我。
t_en: But that stubborn old Krateros has ordered the guards not to let anyone go outside, especially me.

t_ch: 哈哈，不过也情有可原啦。毕竟我要是死了，那可是全人类的损失啊。
t_en: Haha, but I guess it's understandable. After all, if I were to die, that would be a loss for all of humanity!

t_ch: 我帮你问问情况吧。
t_en: I'll help you ask about the situation.

t_ch: 太好了！下届「奥凯纳斯运动会」，我会邀请你坐在掷铁饼比赛的第一排，近距离感受铁饼迎面而来的冲击感！
t_en: Awesome! For the next "Okeanas Games," I'll invite you to sit in the front row of the discus throw event. So you can experience the thrill of a discus flying straight at you up close!

t_ch: 哈哈，举手之劳。
t_en: Haha, it's no trouble at all.

t_ch: 这种感觉还是不要体验了。
t_en: I'd rather not experience that feeling.

t_ch: 你这家伙又来了？就算你这回带上{NICKNAME}阁下一起，我也不会答应你。
t_en: You again? Even if you brought {NICKNAME} along this time, I still won't agree.

t_ch: 光是守住这里就已经十分勉强，更别提抽出人手外出搜救。任由平民独自外出和送死无异，这也是为你着想。
t_en: Just holding this place is already a struggle, let alone sending people out for rescue missions. Letting civilians go out alone is no different from sending them to their deaths, this is for your own good, too.

t_ch: 平民？我早就说了，我才不是什么「平民」。
t_en: Civilians? I've already told you, I'm no "civilian."

t_ch: 我给你讲了我的辉煌战绩，给你看了我健硕的肌肉…我甚至给了你我的签名！
t_en: I've told you all about my glorious achievements, shown you my strong, well-built muscles... I even gave you my autograph!

t_ch: 那很有诚意了。
t_en: That's quite sincere.

t_ch: 谁想要那玩意啊？
t_en: Who even wants that thing?

t_ch: 那、那是你硬塞给我的吧？！
t_en: Th—That was that thing you forced on me, wasn't it!?

t_ch: 抱歉，柯库翁阁下。我认可你在竞技场上的成就，但竞技和战斗不是一回事。我不希望任何人白白送死。
t_en: Sorry, Lord Glykon. I acknowledge your achievements in the arena, but competition and real combat are not the same thing. I don't want anyone to throw their life away needlessly.

t_ch: 如果我陪他一起呢？
t_en: What if I go with him?

t_ch: 如果是{NICKNAME}阁下您一起…您的实力，我自然信任。
t_en: If it's you, {NICKNAME}, going with him... I naturally trust your strength.

t_ch: 太好了，{NICKNAME}！下届「奥凯纳斯运动会」，我会在获奖致谢辞里第一个感谢你。是不是很受宠若惊？
t_en: Fantastic, {NICKNAME}! In the next "Okeanas Games," I'll make sure to thank you first in my victory speech. Feeling honored?

t_ch: 必须写满一整页。
t_en: Make sure to fill an entire page with it!

t_ch: 你还是给我歇歇吧。
t_en: Give it a rest.

t_ch: 卫兵报告，对面圣殿有避难者呼救。那里构造和此处相似，清理盘踞其中的怪物，或许便能建立起一处安全的避难所。
t_en: The guards have reported that there are refugees calling for help from the temple across the way. Its structure is similar to this place. If we clear out the monsters lurking inside, we might be able to establish a safe shelter there.

t_ch: 至少还有两波黑潮即将来袭，去救下难民，赢得你们渴求的荣耀吧，千万小心。
t_en: At least two more waves of the black tide are approaching. Go rescue the refugees and claim the glory you seek, but be extremely careful.

t_ch: 救命…我怕是不行了……
t_en: Help... I don't think I can make it...

t_ch: 别说这种傻话，兄弟！我们不是约好了要一起站上奥赫玛小贩业的顶点吗？
t_en: Don't say such foolish things, brother! Didn't we promise to reach the pinnacle of the Okhema marketplace together?

t_ch: 这些年摆摊攒下的积蓄，我藏在了…藏在了……
t_en: The savings I've gathered from running my stall all these years... I hid them... I hid them...

t_ch: 呃啊……
t_en: Ugh... ah...

t_ch: 你倒是说完啊！
t_en: At least finish what you were saying!

t_ch: 别怕，我来了！
t_en: Have no fear, for I am here!

t_ch: 英雄总在最后一刻救场！
t_en: Heroes always arrive at the last moment to save the day!

t_ch: 啊，是您！{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}开眼，终于有救了！
t_en: Ah, it's you! {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila{RUBY_E#} has opened their eyes — salvation is finally here!

t_ch: 怎么是你们两个？
t_en: Why is it you two?

t_ch: 这事说来话长了——
t_en: That's a long story—

t_ch: 你们是谁来着？
t_en: Who are you guys again?

t_ch: 真是贵人多忘事啊！我们在云石市集摆摊卖矛和盾的时候，还成天看您在街上溜达呢。
t_en: You really do forget people easily! Back when we were running a stall selling spears and shields at the Marmoreal Market, we used to see you strolling around the streets all the time.

t_ch: 呃啊……
t_en: Ugh... ah...

t_ch: 再坚持一会儿，希望就在眼前了！
t_en: Hold on a little longer, hope is right in front of us!

t_ch: 我…实在有点…恐高…呕！
t_en: I... really have a bit of... a fear of heights... ugh!

t_ch: …感觉好些了。
t_en: ...Feeling a bit better now.

t_ch: 呃，这只是二楼啊！你这个样子，以后怎么站上奥赫玛小贩业的顶点？
t_en: Uh, this is just the second floor! With you acting like this, how are you ever going to reach the top of the Okhema marketplace?
